b:T79f2,<div class="specification">
<p>The pedigree chart shows the incidence of hemophilia in some of the descendants of Queen Victoria.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>State the probability that Edward had hemophilia.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Explain the reasons for none of the females in the pedigree chart having hemophilia.</p>
<div class="marks">[3]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/3/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>0.5/50 %;</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<ol style=\"list-style-type:lower-alpha;\">\n<li>hemophilia is X-linked/sex-linked/carried on the X chromosome;</li>\n<li>females have two X chromosomes <br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em> <br/>males only have one X chromosome;</li>\n<li>hemophilia is caused by a recessive allele;</li>\n<li>(trait) must be on both alleles to be expressed <br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em> <br/>females would require the allele on both X chromosomes to have the disease<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>females can be carriers when allele is only on one chromosome;</li>\n</ol>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>Approximately 50 % of the candidates were correct with their answer of 50 %. It was expected that candidates would recognize that Edward is male because he has a box rather than circle in the pedigree. Then from there, they would calculate the 50 %.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>There were many good answers for this question showing understanding of haemophilia being sex linked and recessive Quite a number then failed to get the third mark by being too vague in their explanations. Some candidates thought that hemophilia is carried on Y chromosome; many understand that females have two X chromosomes; talk about females as carriers was often seen, but without any details to expand the answer; or that males are most likely to have hemophilia but, again, no details; for the most part answers showed shallow understanding.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
